query: Klarna P2P peer-to-peer payments European countries launch January 2026

mako.raw:
{"query":"Klarna P2P peer-to-peer payments European countries launch January 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1277,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.paymentsjournal.com/klarna-wades-into-a-crowded-european-p2p-market/","title":"Klarna Wades into a Crowded European P2P Market - PaymentsJournal","content":"Buy now, pay later pioneer Klarna has launched peer-to-peer payments in 13 European countries, expanding its services as it moves closer to becoming a full-service digital bank.
As it expands its services, Klarna already holds a full banking license in the EU.
It has yet to obtain similar status in the U.S., although it launched its IPO in September.
Most European countries offer several local P2P services, and in many markets, the sector has already matured.
Bizum, for example—owned and operated by leading Spanish banks—dominates the P2P market in Spain, one of the 13 countries where Klarna’s service will be available."},{"url":"https://finovate.com/klarna-launches-p2p-payments-in-europe/","title":"Klarna Launches P2P Payments in Europe - Finovate","content":"The Sweden-based company has launched peer-to-peer (P2P) payment capabilities this week, making it possible for users to send funds to family and friends.
The new P2P capabilities will initially be available in 13 European countries: Belgium, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Spain, Sweden, and the UK.
Klarna, which originally launched as a buy-now, pay-later tool in 2005, views this launch as the next step in its evolution as an everyday digital hub for spending and money management.
The launch of the new P2P payment capabilities marks Klarna’s first move in 2026 after a busy 2025, when the company saw the deposits in its Klarna Balance account double from $9.5 billion in 2024 to $14 billion in 2025."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/julieschicktanz_klarna-launches-p2p-payments-in-europe-activity-7417313969490690049--bGj","title":"Klarna Expands to Peer-to-Peer Payments in Europe | Julie Muhn posted on the topic | LinkedIn","content":"🚨 Klarna Is No Longer Just Adding Features, It’s Becoming a Bank Klarna just rolled out peer-to-peer payments across Europe, letting users send money directly inside the Klarna app.
🚨 PAYMENTS: Klarna moves into P2P payments BNPL giant Klarna has launched instant peer-to-peer payments in 13 European countries, marking the next step in its evolution as a digital bank The new feature enables Klarna customers to send money to friends and family, whether splitting bills or gifting cash, directly from the Klarna app.
At launch, transfers work between Klarna users, with plans to soon expand to non-Klarna customers and cross-border payments.
Klarna introduced an instant peer-to-peer payment feature in its app across 13 European markets, including the U.K., Germany, and Sweden.
TrueLayer, an open banking network, partnered with Stripe to launch direct bank payments for Kustom Checkout in Finland.
Klarna launching instant peer‑to‑peer payments across 13 European countries shows how consumer apps are moving deeper into real‑time account‑to‑account payments."}]}

This morning, the Swedish fintech giant launched instant peer-to-peer payments across 13 European countries, enabling customers to send money to friends and family directly from the Klarna app. What might seem like a straightforward feature addition is actually a strategic milestone in Klarna's broader transformation into an everyday banking platform. Users can now send money through the Klarna app using:. Transfers are instant between Klarna users, with real-time fraud and eligibility checks running in the background. The roadmap includes expansion to non-Klarna recipients and cross-border payments—essential capabilities for any platform aspiring to replace traditional banking apps. That's real money sitting in Klarna accounts—not just payment volume flowing through.
